req.title: Beneficiary management
req.statement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period before it can receive transfers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three key behaviours: (1) a valid beneficiary POST results in HTTP 201 with status='pending' and a cooling_off_until timestamp ~24 h in the future; (2) posting the same account a second time yields HTTP 409; (3) the cooling-off boundary — attempting a transfer exactly at the 24-hour mark (or just before) must still be blocked, while just after must succeed. Tests are scoped to the /beneficiaries endpoint. Each test uses a unique account number derived from a UUID so the suite is idempotent across repeated runs. Fixtures supply the authenticated HTTP session; helper stubs replace verbose setup code.

tests.name: test_add_beneficiary_returns_201_and_pending_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_token> fixture)
Account number does not already exist for this customer
actions: Generate a unique account number with <unique_account_number>
POST /beneficiaries with valid payload {account_number, name, bank_code}
Capture response status code and body
expected: Response body contains a cooling_off_until timestamp approximately 24 hours from now (within a 60-second tolerance)
Response body contains the submitted account_number
trace: R-FIN-011: beneficiary saved in pending state
R-FIN-011: 24-hour cooling-off period begins on add
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from dateutil.parser import parse as parse_dt

def test_add_beneficiary_returns_201_and_pending_state(api_session, unique_account_number):
    payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account_number,
        "name": "Alice Tester",
        "bank_code": "TESTGB22"
    }
    resp = api_session.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body["account_number"] == unique_account_number

    cooling_off = parse_dt(body["cooling_off_until"])
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    expected_expiry = now + timedelta(hours=24)
    delta = abs((cooling_off - expected_expiry).total_seconds())
    assert delta <= 60, f"cooling_off_until off by {delta}s"

tests.name: test_add_duplicate_beneficiary_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_token> fixture)
A beneficiary with the target account number already exists for this customer
actions: Generate a unique account number with <unique_account_number>
POST /beneficiaries with that account number (first call — seeds the duplicate)
POST /beneficiaries again with the identical account number
Capture second response status code and body
expected: Response body contains an error code or message indicating duplicate account
trace: R-FIN-011: Error pathway — 409 if duplicate account
tests.executable:
def test_add_duplicate_beneficiary_returns_409(api_session, unique_account_number):
    payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account_number,
        "name": "Bob Duplicate",
        "bank_code": "TESTGB22"
    }
    first = api_session.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload)
    assert first.status_code == 201, f"Seed POST failed: {first.status_code}"

    second = api_session.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload)
    assert second.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {second.status_code}: {second.text}"

    body = second.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "409 body must contain error detail"
    error_text = str(body.get("error", "") + body.get("message", "")).lower()
    assert "duplicate" in error_text or "already exists" in error_text or "conflict" in error_text

tests.name: test_transfer_blocked_before_cooling_off_expires
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_token> fixture)
A beneficiary has been added and is still within the 24-hour cooling-off window
actions: Generate a unique account number with <unique_account_number>
POST /beneficiaries to create beneficiary (status should be 'pending')
Immediately attempt POST /transfers to the newly created beneficiary
Capture response status code and body
expected: Beneficiary creation returns HTTP 201 with status='pending'
Transfer attempt returns HTTP 403 or HTTP 422 (transfer blocked during cooling-off)
Response body indicates cooling-off restriction is in effect
trace: R-FIN-011: beneficiary cannot receive transfers during 24-hour cooling-off period
R-FIN-011: boundary — t=0 (just added) is inside the restricted window
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_blocked_before_cooling_off_expires(api_session, unique_account_number):
    ben_payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account_number,
        "name": "Carol Boundary",
        "bank_code": "TESTGB22"
    }
    ben_resp = api_session.post("/beneficiaries", json=ben_payload)
    assert ben_resp.status_code == 201
    beneficiary_id = ben_resp.json()["id"]
    assert ben_resp.json()["status"] == "pending"

    transfer_payload = {
        "beneficiary_id": beneficiary_id,
        "amount": "1.00",
        "currency": "GBP",
        "reference": "BVA-boundary-test"
    }
    tr_resp = api_session.post("/transfers", json=transfer_payload)
    assert tr_resp.status_code in (403, 422), (
        f"Expected 403 or 422 during cooling-off, got {tr_resp.status_code}: {tr_resp.text}"
    )
    body = tr_resp.json()
    body_str = str(body).lower()
    assert "cooling" in body_str or "pending" in body_str or "restricted" in body_str

tests.name: test_beneficiary_state_transitions_pending_to_active
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_token> fixture)
Test environment supports time-travel or admin override to advance cooling-off clock via <advance_cooling_off> helper
actions: Generate a unique account number with <unique_account_number>
Invoke <advance_cooling_off>(beneficiary_id, hours=24) to expire the cooling-off period
POST /transfers to the beneficiary — expect 2xx (transfer now allowed)
expected: After advancing clock 24 h, GET returns status='active'
Transfer to active beneficiary returns 200 or 201
trace: R-FIN-011: 24-hour cooling-off period — state transition pending -> active
R-FIN-011: beneficiary can receive transfers after cooling-off expires
tests.executable:
def test_beneficiary_state_transitions_pending_to_active(
    api_session, unique_account_number, advance_cooling_off
):
    payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account_number,
        "name": "Dave Transition",
        "bank_code": "TESTGB22"
    }
    create_resp = api_session.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    body = create_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    beneficiary_id = body["id"]

    advance_cooling_off(beneficiary_id, hours=24)

    get_resp = api_session.get(f"/beneficiaries/{beneficiary_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active"

    transfer_payload = {
        "beneficiary_id": beneficiary_id,
        "amount": "10.00",
        "currency": "GBP",
        "reference": "STC-post-cooling-off"
    }
    tr_resp = api_session.post("/transfers", json=transfer_payload)
    assert tr_resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Transfer should succeed after cooling-off; got {tr_resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_add_beneficiary_missing_required_fields_returns_400
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_token> fixture)
actions: POST /beneficiaries with each of the following incomplete payloads in turn: (a) missing account_number, (b) missing name, (c) missing bank_code
Capture status code and error body for each call
expected: All three POST calls return HTTP 400 Bad Request
Each response body identifies the missing field
trace: R-FIN-011: precondition — valid beneficiary data required before cooling-off can start
DT rule: any required field absent => reject before persisting
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("payload,missing_field", [
    ({"name": "Eve Test", "bank_code": "TESTGB22"}, "account_number"),
    ({"account_number": "GB00TEST12345601", "bank_code": "TESTGB22"}, "name"),
    ({"account_number": "GB00TEST12345602", "name": "Frank Test"}, "bank_code"),
])
def test_add_beneficiary_missing_required_fields_returns_400(
    api_session, payload, missing_field
):
    resp = api_session.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, (
        f"Expected 400 for missing {missing_field}, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body_str = str(resp.json()).lower()
    assert missing_field.replace("_", "") in body_str.replace("_", "") or \
           missing_field in body_str, \
           f"Response should reference missing field '{missing_field}'"
